title: Your Personalized Recommendations
intro: Based on your responses, here are some recommendations to support your health and well-being. You can share these with your healthcare provider.

high_priority.heading: We Recommend Testing Today
high_priority.message: Based on your responses, STI testing is recommended. Testing is quick, confidential, and an important step in caring for your health. Your healthcare provider can help you decide which tests are right for you.
actions: Ask your provider about testing for chlamydia, gonorrhea, and syphilis
Testing is usually a simple urine test or blood draw
Results are typically available within a few days

moderate_priority.heading: Testing Is Recommended
moderate_priority.message: Regular STI testing is an important part of your healthcare. Even if you feel healthy, testing helps ensure early detection and treatment.
actions: Talk to your provider about which STI tests are appropriate for you
Consider making testing part of your routine health visits
Your provider can answer any questions about the testing process

pregnancy_priority.heading: Testing Is Important for Your Pregnancy
pregnancy_priority.message: STI testing during pregnancy is especially important to protect your health and your baby's health. Syphilis screening is particularly critical, as it can be passed to your baby and cause serious complications.
actions: Ask your provider about syphilis screening if you haven't been tested
Testing and treatment during pregnancy protects your baby
Your prenatal care team can coordinate all recommended testing

routine.heading: Keep Up the Good Work
routine.message: It sounds like you've been staying on top of your sexual health. Continue to get tested regularly and talk openly with your healthcare provider.
actions: Continue regular testing as part of your routine care
Encourage friends and partners to get tested too

condom_use.heading: Barrier Method Support
condom_use.message: Condoms and other barrier methods are effective at reducing STI risk. If you'd like to use them more consistently, your healthcare provider can help.

prep_info.heading: HIV Prevention (PrEP)
prep_info.message: PrEP is a medication that can prevent HIV. Ask your provider if PrEP might be right for you.

mental_health.heading: Mental Health Support
mental_health.message: Your emotional well-being matters. Support services are available and seeking help is a sign of strength.

substance_use.heading: Substance Use Support
substance_use.message: If you're concerned about substance use, confidential support services are available. You don't have to face this alone.

safety.heading: Safety Resources
safety.message: Everyone deserves to feel safe. Confidential support is available 24/7.

basic_needs.heading: Community Resources
basic_needs.message: There are resources available to help with food, housing, transportation, and other needs in your community.

providerSection.title: Talk to Your Provider
providerSection.message: Your healthcare provider is here to help. You can share this summary with them to start the conversation. Remember, they are required to keep your information confidential.
tips: You can share your TRUST-STI summary with your provider
It's okay to ask questions about anything you don't understand
You have the right to make your own decisions about your health
If you're not comfortable with your provider, you can ask to see someone else
